title: Information And Communication Technology Application As A Solution In The Fight Against Terrorism In Developing Countries: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Umaru, Zubairu Yandayi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This repository contains the complete replication materials and structured datasets supporting the systematic literature review titled:  Information And Communication Technology Application As A Solution In The Fight Against Terrorism In Developing Countries: A Systematic Literature Review  
 The study examines the role of Information and Communication Technology (ICT) infrastructures in combating terrorism, with particular attention to developing-country contexts. The review was conducted using a structured PRISMA-based methodology and includes analytical synthesis using the ADO–TCM framework. 
 The repository includes: 
 • Review protocol summary • Complete search strategies and Boolean query strings used across databases • Structured database retrieval summaries • PRISMA flow stage counts • Inclusion and exclusion criteria • Screening log (screening and full-text eligibility stages) • Quality assessment framework and scoring sheet • Data extraction form • Extracted dataset of the 31 included studies • ADO–TCM analytical coding dataset 
 Due to licensing restrictions imposed by subscription-based databases (e.g., Scopus, Web of Science), raw database export files cannot be publicly redistributed. However, complete search strategies and structured processed datasets are provided to ensure methodological transparency and enable reproducibility for researchers with database access. 
 There are no additional restrictions on the use of the shared materials.
